description_long: This kiosk is designed as a historical artillery display, featuring a large, ornately decorated bronze cannon mounted on a robust wooden carriage with metal reinforcements and wheels. The cannon sits atop a weathered, rectangular metal platform, which is set on a marble base. To one side, there are two long wooden poles, possibly ramrods or cleaning tools, displayed within a low, fenced-off area. A black cannonball and a wedge are positioned near the cannon, adding to the authenticity of the exhibit. The area is cordoned off with black stanchions and a sign prohibiting photography, while the marble base is marked with red warning text. The overall presentation is reminiscent of a museum or heritage park installation, emphasizing historical and educational value.
description: A historical cannon kiosk display with a bronze cannon on a wooden carriage, set on a marble base with tools, signage, and safety barriers.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cannon's barrel, ornate details, the wooden carriage, cannonball, wedge, and the no-photography sign prominently.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the length of the cannon, the two long poles in their display area, and the arrangement of the stanchions and signage.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back of the cannon, carriage wheels, and the cannonball, with the signpost visible behind the display.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focuses on the cannon's rear, the marble base, and the full length of the tool display area with stanchions.
attribute_signage_type: Printed sign with a camera and prohibition symbol, mounted on a pole at the front left corner; red warning text printed on the marble base.
